Keywords: responsive, mobile, tablet, breakpoints, viewport, cross-device, touch targets
Description: Adapt designs across screen sizes, devices, and platforms
When To Use: User mentions responsive design or cross-device compatibility
Key Steps: 1. Identify source context 2. Understand target context 3. Plan adaptation strategy 4. Implement layout/touch/content/nav adaptations 5. Test on real devices
Critical Rules: Never hide core functionality on mobile; always test real devices; use content-driven breakpoints

Keywords: animation, transitions, micro-interactions, motion, hover effects, alive
When To Use: User wants animations or mentions UI feeling static
Key Steps: 1. Identify static areas 2. Understand context 3. Plan hero moment + feedback + transition + delight layers 4. Implement with proper timing/easing 5. Verify 60fps + reduced motion
Critical Rules: Never bounce/elastic easing; only animate transform/opacity; respect prefers-reduced-motion

Keywords: layout, spacing, visual hierarchy, crowded, alignment, composition
Description: Improve layout, spacing, and visual rhythm
When To Use: Layout feels off or spacing is inconsistent
Key Steps: 1. Assess spacing/hierarchy/grid/rhythm/density 2. Plan spacing system 3. Establish consistent scale 4. Create visual rhythm 5. Choose right layout tool 6. Verify with squint test
Critical Rules: Never use arbitrary spacing; never wrap everything in cards; never nest cards; never center everything by default

Keywords: accessibility, performance, theming, responsive, quality, a11y
Description: Run technical quality checks across 5 dimensions
When To Use: User wants accessibility check or technical audit
Key Steps: 1. Run diagnostics across a11y/performance/responsive/theming/anti-patterns 2. Score each 0-4 3. Generate report with P0-P3 severity 4. Recommend actions
Critical Rules: Document issues don't fix them; check ALL anti-pattern guidelines; be thorough but actionable

Keywords: bland, generic, safe, personality, impact, character, boring
Description: Amplify safe designs to be more visually interesting
When To Use: Design looks bland or lacks personality
Key Steps: 1. Identify weakness sources 2. Understand context limits 3. Plan focal point + personality direction 4. Amplify typography/color/spatial/effects/motion 5. Verify not AI slop
Critical Rules: Bold != chaotic; watch for AI slop traps; maintain usability; one focal point

Keywords: confusing text, unclear labels, error messages, UX writing, instructions
Description: Improve unclear UX copy and microcopy
When To Use: Confusing text or poor error messages
Key Steps: 1. Find clarity problems 2. Understand audience context 3. Improve error messages/labels/buttons/help text/empty states 4. Apply 6 clarity principles
Critical Rules: Never blame users; never use jargon without explanation; be specific not vague; consistent terminology

Keywords: gray, dull, monochromatic, warmth, vibrant, color, palette
Description: Add strategic color to monochromatic designs
When To Use: Design looks gray or lacks visual warmth
Key Steps: 1. Assess current color state 2. Identify where color adds value 3. Plan dominant/accent colors (2-4 max) 4. Apply semantic/accent/background/data color 5. Maintain 60-30-10 balance
Critical Rules: More color != better; never gray on colored backgrounds; never pure gray/black/white; WCAG contrast required

Keywords: review, evaluate, feedback, UX critique, design review
When To Use: User asks to review or critique a design
Key Steps: 1. AI slop detection 2. Evaluate 10 dimensions 3. Score Nielsen's 10 heuristics 0-4 4. Present findings with P0-P3 severity 5. Persona red flags 6. Recommend actions
Critical Rules: Be brutally honest; be specific not vague; prioritize ruthlessly; check ALL anti-patterns

Keywords: joy, personality, fun, memorable, micro-interactions, polish
Description: Add moments of joy and personality
When To Use: User wants more personality or fun in UI
Key Steps: 1. Find natural delight moments 2. Define delight strategy 3. Add micro-interactions/personality copy/illustrations/sounds/easter eggs 4. Verify appropriate and performant
Critical Rules: Delight enhances never blocks; never delay core functionality; never overdo; appropriate to context

Keywords: components, design tokens, patterns, design system, refactor
Description: Extract reusable components and design tokens
When To Use: User wants to create components or design system
Key Steps: 1. Find design system 2. Identify repeated patterns 3. Assess extraction value (3+ uses) 4. Extract components with proper API/a11y 5. Create tokens 6. Migrate uses 7. Document
Critical Rules: Don't extract one-offs; don't create overly generic components; respect existing conventions

Keywords: production-ready, edge cases, error states, i18n, overflow
When To Use: Making code production-ready or handling edge cases
Key Steps: 1. Test extreme inputs 2. Test error scenarios 3. Test i18n 4. Handle text overflow 5. Handle errors gracefully 6. Handle large datasets 7. Verify accessibility resilience
Critical Rules: Never assume perfect input; validate everything; use Intl API for dates/numbers; use logical CSS properties for RTL

Keywords: consistency, design drift, mismatched styles, tokens, standards
Description: Realign UI to match design system standards
When To Use: Features have drifted from design system
Key Steps: 1. Discover design system 2. Analyze deviations 3. Plan normalization 4. Fix typography/color/spacing/components/motion/responsive/a11y 5. Clean up code
Critical Rules: Never create one-off components when design system equivalents exist; never hard-code values

Keywords: onboarding, first-time, empty states, activation, getting started
Description: Design onboarding flows and first-run experiences
When To Use: User mentions onboarding or empty states
Key Steps: 1. Identify user challenge 2. Define success metrics 3. Design welcome/setup/core concepts/first success 4. Create empty states 5. Add contextual help
Critical Rules: Get to value ASAP; teach 20% that delivers 80% value; make skippable; show don't tell

Keywords: slow, laggy, janky, performance, bundle size, load time
Description: Diagnose and fix UI performance issues
When To Use: User mentions slowness or performance problems
Key Steps: 1. Measure Core Web Vitals 2. Identify bottlenecks 3. Optimize images/JS bundle/CSS/fonts 4. Fix rendering performance 5. Fix animation performance 6. Monitor
Critical Rules: Measure before optimizing; never sacrifice accessibility; optimize biggest bottleneck first; test on real devices

Keywords: wow, impress, extraordinary, ambitious, go all-out
Key Steps: 1. Assess what extraordinary means for context 2. Propose 2-3 directions 3. Get user confirmation 4. Implement with progressive enhancement 5. Iterate visually
Critical Rules: Always progressive enhancement; always prefers-reduced-motion; always fallbacks; propose before building

Keywords: finishing touches, pre-launch, looks off, good to great
When To Use: Pre-launch review or something looks off
Key Steps: 1. Review completeness 2. Fix alignment/spacing 3. Refine typography 4. Check all interaction states 5. Smooth transitions 6. Fix edge cases 7. Clean code
Critical Rules: Polish is last step not first; don't polish incomplete features; zoom in on details; test real devices

Keywords: too bold, overwhelming, aggressive, garish, calmer, refined
When To Use: Design is too bold or overwhelming
Key Steps: 1. Identify intensity sources 2. Reduce color saturation 3. Reduce visual weight 4. Simplify decorative elements 5. Reduce motion intensity 6. Refine composition
Critical Rules: Quieter != boring; maintain hierarchy; keep some personality; restraint requires precision

Keywords: fonts, type, readability, text hierarchy, sizing, typography
When To Use: Fonts or text hierarchy looks off
Key Steps: 1. Assess font choices 2. Check hierarchy 3. Check sizing scale 4. Fix readability 5. Establish consistent scale 6. Refine weight/spacing/kerning
Critical Rules: Never >2-3 font families; never body text <16px; never px for font sizes; never pair similar fonts
